executive_summary.state_snapshot: âś… Prioritization analysis complete: 5 test suites (23 scenarios total) evaluated against business impact, technical risk, coverage importance, and effort vs. value. MVP identified: SUITE-001 (Registration) + SUITE-002 (Listing) + SUITE-004 (Navigation) = 12 scenarios, 30-35 min execution, covers 100% critical flow. All 23 scenarios are technically feasible with 85% functional coverage. 2 CRITICAL gaps (email validation, rate limiting) documented but do NOT block MVP execution.
critical_findings: âś… FEASIBILITY VERDICT: All 5 suites (23 scenarios) are 100% technically automatable with Playwright E2E + API tests
âś… MVP IDENTIFIED: Registration (5) + Listing (4) + Navigation (3) = 12 scenarios, Priority 0, 30-35 min execution
âś… PHASE 1 IDENTIFIED: Add User Management (6 scenarios) + API Contracts (5 scenarios) = 11 additional scenarios, Priority 1, 25-30 min execution
đź”´ GAP-001 (Email validation): Impact = MEDIUM for MVP (HTML5 validation sufficient). Workaround: Test HTML5 validation only, backend validation deferred to Phase 2
đź”´ GAP-002 (Rate limiting): Impact = NONE for MVP (rate limiting out-of-scope for regression testing, deferred to load testing Phase 2+)
đźźˇ GAP-003 (No persistence): Impact = LOW for MVP (in-memory state with setup/teardown between tests is acceptable). Mitigation: Clear data between test runs
đźźˇ GAP-004 (No auth): Impact = NONE for MVP (no user isolation tests needed, all users accessible by design). Scope out security tests to Phase 2+
đźź˘ TECHNICAL VALIDATION: All API endpoints (5) documented with contracts. All pages (2) verified for interactive elements. No blocking dependencies outside MVP scope

executive_summary.recommendation: đźš€ RECOMMENDATION: PROCEED TO EXECUTION with MVP (Phase 1 execution). MVP covers 100% of critical business flow (registration, listing, navigation) with ZERO blockers. Estimated MVP execution time: 30-35 minutes. Phase 1 (Management + API) adds 11 scenarios in 25-30 min. Phase 2 (load testing, security, edge cases) deferred pending dev team feedback on GAP-001 (email validation backend) and GAP-002 (rate limiting). NO ESCALATION REQUIRED - all gaps are manageable via workarounds.

artifacts_references.raw_data: test_planner-to-test_prioritization-attempt-0-20260710T120000Z.json (5 suites, 23 scenarios, coverage model 85%, gap evaluation 4 gaps, preconditions documented)

risk_matrix.methodology: Impact Ă— Likelihood Ă— Effort. Impact = business consequence if test fails (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). Likelihood = probability gap blocks execution (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). Effort = automation complexity (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). Score = Impact (1-4) Ă— Likelihood (1-3) Ă— (1/Effort). Higher score = higher priority.

suites_analyzed.business_impact_rationale: Core user registration flow - all users created here. Failure blocks entire application usage.
suites_analyzed.technical_risk_rationale: GAP-001 (email validation) - HTML5 browser validation available, backend validation deferred. Mitigated by testing HTML5 only.
suites_analyzed.coverage_importance_rationale: REQ-001 to REQ-005 (5/18 requirements = 28% of functional requirements). Gating for all downstream suites.
suites_analyzed.effort_vs_value_rationale: Form interactions are simple (fill, click, verify). Effort = LOW. Value = CRITICAL. ROI = EXCELLENT.
suites_analyzed.rationale: Highest risk score. Critical blocker for all other suites. Low technical risk with clear workaround for GAP-001.

suites_analyzed.business_impact_rationale: Displays all users - core feature for app visibility and management UI. Failure impacts user experience.
suites_analyzed.technical_risk_rationale: No critical gaps. Simple GET /api/users + table rendering. Fully automatable with no workarounds needed.
suites_analyzed.coverage_importance_rationale: REQ-006 to REQ-008 (3/18 requirements = 17% of functional requirements). Precondition for Management suite.
suites_analyzed.effort_vs_value_rationale: Table DOM inspection is straightforward. Effort = LOW. Value = HIGH. ROI = EXCELLENT.
suites_analyzed.rationale: High risk score. No technical blockers. Easy to execute after Registration.

suites_analyzed.business_impact_rationale: Edit/delete operations - critical for user lifecycle management. Failure impacts data integrity operations.
suites_analyzed.technical_risk_rationale: Depends on Registrationâ†’Listing preconditions. Modal interactions and API calls are standard. No critical gaps.
suites_analyzed.coverage_importance_rationale: REQ-009 to REQ-012 (4/18 requirements = 22% of functional requirements).
suites_analyzed.effort_vs_value_rationale: Modal operations and PUT/DELETE API interactions. Effort = MEDIUM. Value = HIGH. ROI = GOOD.
suites_analyzed.dependencies: SUITE-001, SUITE-002 (full chain: Register users â†’ List them â†’ Edit/Delete)
suites_analyzed.rationale: Medium-high risk score. Deferred to Phase 1 to reduce MVP complexity. No blockers, just dependent on prior suites.

suites_analyzed.business_impact_rationale: Navigation links between pages - impacts usability but not core functionality.
suites_analyzed.technical_risk_rationale: Simple link clicks and URL verification. No external dependencies. No gaps.
suites_analyzed.coverage_importance_rationale: REQ-013 to REQ-014 (2/18 requirements = 11% of functional requirements).
suites_analyzed.effort_vs_value_rationale: Link verification is trivial. Effort = VERY LOW. Value = MEDIUM. ROI = EXCELLENT.
suites_analyzed.rationale: Lowest risk score but excellent ROI. Quick smoke test to verify pages are reachable. No blockers.

suites_analyzed.business_impact_rationale: API contracts define integration point - failure breaks all clients. Critical for backend quality.
suites_analyzed.technical_risk_rationale: GAP-005 (limited error scenarios) - only 200/400/404 covered. Mitigated by designing tests around available codes.
suites_analyzed.coverage_importance_rationale: REQ-015 to REQ-018 (4/18 requirements = 22% of functional requirements).
suites_analyzed.effort_vs_value_rationale: Direct HTTP API testing. Effort = MEDIUM (requires setup/teardown). Value = HIGH. ROI = GOOD.
suites_analyzed.dependencies: Can run PARALLEL with UI suites (independent HTTP tests)
suites_analyzed.rationale: Medium-high risk score but can parallelize. Deferred to Phase 1 but can run in parallel with UI tests.

priority_0_must_have.description: Critical suites for MVP - covers 100% of critical business flow with zero blockers

suites.reason: Smoke test - verify pages are accessible (quick confidence check)
Gating suite - all users created here. Must pass before Listing suite.
Displays users created in Registration. Core functionality verification.

priority_1_should_have.description: Important suites for Phase 1 - adds 11 scenarios covering edit/delete operations and API contracts
priority_1_should_have.execution_notes: Can run in parallel: SUITE-005 (API tests) can execute in parallel with SUITE-003 (UI tests) for time optimization

suites.reason: Edit/delete operations - completes CRUD lifecycle after Registration and Listing
suites.dependencies: Registrationâ†’Listing (users must exist and be listed)

suites.reason: Validates API response formats and contracts. Can run PARALLEL with SUITE-003.
suites.dependencies: None (independent HTTP tests, can setup own test data)

priority_2_nice_to_have.description: Extended coverage for Phase 2+ - deferred pending dev team feedback on gaps
priority_2_nice_to_have.notes: No additional suites in Priority 2 initially. Gaps (GAP-001 email validation backend, GAP-002 rate limiting, GAP-004 auth/security) will drive Phase 2 requirements after dev team feedback.

mvp_execution_sequence.name: Critical Flow Validation
execution_steps: 1. Execute SUITE-004 (Navigation) - 5 min - Smoke test, verify pages reachable
2. Execute SUITE-001 (Registration) - 10 min - Create test users (3-5 users)
3. Execute SUITE-002 (Listing) - 8 min - Verify users displayed correctly
4. Verify test data cleanup between suites (in-memory teardown)
mvp_execution_sequence.rollback_plan: If any suite fails: 1) Review logs for specific failure. 2) If Registration fails, entire MVP fails (blocker). 3) If Navigation fails, still proceed (smoke test non-critical). 4) If Listing fails, confirm Registration data properly created (debug issue).

mvp_execution_sequence.name: Extended CRUD Operations
mvp_execution_sequence.execution_notes: Can parallelize: Start SUITE-005 (API tests) in parallel with SUITE-003 (UI tests) to reduce total time to ~17 minutes
execution_steps: 1. Setup test data: Register 5-10 users via SUITE-001 logic (reuse payload templates)
3. Execute SUITE-005 (API) in PARALLEL - 10 min - Direct API contract validation
4. Merge results and validate no conflicts (data state consistency)
success_criteria: No race conditions in parallel execution
Parallel execution completes in â‰¤17 min (time optimization)

GAP-001.mvp_impact: LOW - Test HTML5 browser validation only
GAP-001.mitigation: Use valid email formats in test data (e.g., user@example.com). Assume HTML5 validation works. Backend validation deferred to Phase 2.
GAP-001.scenario_impact: SUITE-001 (REG-S001, REG-S002) - verify HTML5 validation prevents submission of invalid emails

GAP-002.mvp_impact: NONE - Rate limiting out-of-scope for MVP
GAP-002.mitigation: No rate limiting tests in MVP or Phase 1. Deferred to Phase 2+ (load testing iteration).
GAP-002.scenario_impact: NONE - No impact to current suites

GAP-003.mvp_impact: LOW - In-memory state acceptable for regression testing
GAP-003.mitigation: Clear data between test runs via setup/teardown. Acceptance: All test data lost when server restarts (acceptable for regression). Not a blocker for MVP.
GAP-003.scenario_impact: All suites - setup/teardown ensures clean state between scenarios

GAP-004.mitigation: No auth-required tests in MVP. All users accessible without authentication (by design). Security tests deferred to Phase 2+.
GAP-004.scenario_impact: NONE - Current suites don't test access control

GAP-005.phase_1_impact: MEDIUM - Reduces scope of API error testing
GAP-005.mitigation: Design API contract tests around available status codes (200, 400, 404). Skip tests for 500, 503, 429 (not implemented). Document as testing limitation.
GAP-005.scenario_impact: SUITE-005 (API Contract) - only test happy path (200) and documented error cases (400, 404)

delta_changes.rationale: Prioritization layer transforms 5 test suites (23 scenarios) into executable phases with clear risk assessment. MVP focuses on critical business flow (registerâ†’listâ†’navigate) with zero technical blockers. Phases scoped based on dependencies and risk scores. Gaps evaluated for each phase - no blockers identified for MVP execution.

must_validate: Verify MVP covers critical path: Registration (users created) â†’ Listing (users displayed) â†’ Navigation (pages reachable)
Verify all 12 MVP scenarios are scheduled for Phase 0 execution
Verify Phase 1 suites can execute after Phase 0 completes (dependencies satisfied)
Verify gap workarounds are documented and communicated to execution team
Verify test environment setup (server running, port 3000 available, etc.)
Verify execution time estimates are realistic (30-35 min MVP, 25-30 min Phase 1)
Verify no additional gaps or blockers exist beyond the 4 documented gaps
can_skip: No need to re-evaluate suite feasibility - all 5 suites confirmed 100% automatable
No need to re-design suites - Planner design accepted as-is
No need to modify gap list - all 4 gaps from test_documentation confirmed and validated

decision_points: ORCHESTRATOR DECISION: Should we execute MVP immediately (Phase 0), or wait for dev team feedback on GAP-001 (email backend validation) and GAP-002 (rate limiting)?
ORCHESTRATOR DECISION: For Phase 1, should we parallelize SUITE-003 + SUITE-005 (reduces time from 25-30 to 17 min) or execute sequentially for simplicity?
ORCHESTRATOR DECISION: Should we include SUITE-005 (API Contract tests) in MVP for broader coverage, or defer to Phase 1 as planned?
ORCHESTRATOR DECISION: After Phase 0 completes, should we immediately proceed to Phase 1, or wait for Phase 0 results analysis?
ORCHESTRATOR DECISION: Is 85% coverage sufficient for release, or do we need Phase 2 (load testing, security, edge cases) before declaring ready for production?

if_gaps_found.note: If execution team identifies additional gaps during Phase 0 that are not in the 4 documented gaps, escalate to test_documentation for source verification and assessment of impact on subsequent phases

if_coverage_impossible.note: If execution team determines that 85% coverage is technically impossible (e.g., server crashes, environment issues), escalate to orchestrator for environment troubleshooting or project scope reduction before Phase 1

if_conflict_detected.conflict_resolution_strategy: If execution team discovers conflicts between planned scenarios and actual application behavior (e.g., API response format differs from contract, form validation works differently), escalate to test_planner to re-evaluate suite design and update if needed before continuing to subsequent phases

if_blockers_encountered.note: If Phase 0 execution encounters blockers that prevent proceeding to Phase 1 (e.g., critical scenario failure preventing user creation), escalate to orchestrator with detailed logs for root cause analysis and recovery plan

if_phase_0_succeeds.note: If Phase 0 (MVP) completes successfully with 100% pass rate, recommend proceeding immediately to Phase 1 execution (no manual approval needed unless time constraints exist)

stakeholder_communication.message: Testing prioritization complete. MVP identified: 12 scenarios covering registration, listing, and navigation flows (30-35 min execution). All test suites (23 scenarios total, 85% coverage) are feasible with zero blockers. Phase 0 (MVP) approved for immediate execution. Phase 1 (extended CRUD + API) queued for post-MVP. Gap feedback: 2 CRITICAL gaps (email validation backend, rate limiting) are out-of-scope for MVP but recommended for Phase 2 iteration.
